Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice grains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination)
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter (exact ESP for this gene not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone, the entry intermediate of flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies early precursor pool for anthocyanin production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm; absence associated with lighter purple grains and lower anthocyanin content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic stacking in rice; PCR genotyping of lines missing SsCHS; phenotype comparison; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; HPLC anthocyanin quantification; TEAC assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHS was one of six Coleus structural genes used to reconstruct the anthocyanin pathway in rice endosperm. 2. Although endogenous OsCHS was activated by the transgenic regulators, lines lacking SsCHS showed lighter purple grains. 3. This indicates the transgene supplemented endogenous function and enhanced anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Can be used in multigene biofortification strategies to boost anthocyanin production in cereal endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of stacked transgene set associated with ~25% reduced grain weight in PER lines; gene-specific effect not isolated
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at 7 and 14 DAP; endosperm-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data used for endogenous pathway context; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text formatting sometimes uses Coleus as source and mentions corresponding endogenous OsCHS activation. Study species is rice, origin species is Coleus.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice grains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination)
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter (exact ESP for this gene not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes chalcone to flavanone in the early flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor for downstream anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation in rice endosperm; absence associated with lighter purple grains and lower anthocyanin content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic stacking in rice; PCR genotyping of lines missing SsCHI; phenotype comparison; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; HPLC anthocyanin quantification; TEAC assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsCHI was introduced as part of the complete structural gene set for anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. Endogenous OsCHI was activated in PER, but lines lacking SsCHI still had lighter purple grains. 3. Thus SsCHI supplementation enhanced final anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful component for engineering complete flavonoid pathways in cereal endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of stacked transgene set associated with ~25% reduced grain weight in PER lines; gene-specific effect not isolated
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at 7 and 14 DAP; endosperm-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data used for endogenous pathway context; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accession listed in paper as ScCHI in Accessions section but gene discussed throughout as SsCHI; extracted as study focal gene SsCHI.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice grains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination)
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter (exact ESP for this gene not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones at the 3-position to form dihydroflavonols for downstream anthocyanin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors for DFR and anthocyanidin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to complete anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm because endogenous OsF3H was not activated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic stacking in rice; semi-quantitative RT-PCR showing endogenous OsF3H not activated; HPLC anthocyanin quantification; purple endosperm phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3H was transferred because endogenous OsF3H was not expressed in wild-type or PER endosperm. 2. Its transgenic expression helped complete the anthocyanin pathway in rice endosperm. 3. This complementation contributed to production of C3G and P3G in PER grains.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Important complementation gene for reconstructing anthocyanin biosynthesis in white-endosperm rice
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of stacked transgene set associated with ~25% reduced grain weight in PER lines; gene-specific effect not isolated
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at 7 and 14 DAP; endosperm-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data used for endogenous pathway context; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly states OsF3H was not activated by the regulatory genes in both ZH11 and PER-Z#3, justifying need for SsF3H.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice grains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination)
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter (exact ESP for this gene not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavanones or dihydroflavonols in the pelargonidin/cyanidin branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a 3'-hydroxyl group into flavonoid intermediates, directing flux toward cyanidin-derived anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directs branch flux toward cyanidin/P3G/C3G products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch enzyme; specific control strength not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation and cyanidin-derived product formation in rice endosperm; absence associated with lighter purple grains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic stacking in rice; PCR genotyping of lines missing SsF3'H; phenotype comparison; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; HPLC anthocyanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsF3'H was included to support cyanidin-type anthocyanin biosynthesis in engineered rice endosperm. 2. Although endogenous OsF3'H was activated, lines lacking the transgene had lighter purple grains. 3. This indicates the transgene strengthened pathway flux toward the detected cyanidin-derived anthocyanins C3G and P3G.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for directing anthocyanin branch composition in biofortified rice grains
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of stacked transgene set associated with ~25% reduced grain weight in PER lines; gene-specific effect not isolated
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at 7 and 14 DAP; endosperm-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data used for endogenous pathway context; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper formatting renders the gene as SsF30 H or SsF3'0H; interpreted as SsF3'H (flavonoid 3'-hydroxylase).

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice grains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination)
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter (exact ESP for this gene not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins for subsequent anthocyanidin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for anthocyanidins and final anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required step in this study because endogenous OsDFR is defective
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for purple endosperm formation; absence abolished purple seed phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence analysis showing endogenous OsDFR defective; stable transgenic stacking in rice; PCR genotyping of line lacking SsDFR; phenotype comparison showing no purple seeds; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; HPLC anthocyanin quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endogenous OsDFR is defective in the recipient rice varieties. 2. SsDFR was introduced to complement this missing activity. 3. A line lacking SsDFR did not produce purple seeds, confirming SsDFR is essential for engineered anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Critical target for restoring anthocyanin biosynthesis in nonpigmented rice endosperm and for metabolic biofortification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of stacked transgene set associated with ~25% reduced grain weight in PER lines; gene-specific effect not isolated
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at 7 and 14 DAP; endosperm-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data used for endogenous pathway context; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the most decisive structural genes because endogenous OsDFR is defective in ZH11 and HG1.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice grains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination)
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter (exact ESP for this gene not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins, enabling formation of glycosylated anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms anthocyanidin precursor of the detected final anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required late step; specific rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to complete anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm because endogenous OsANS was not activated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic stacking in rice; semi-quantitative RT-PCR showing endogenous OsANS not activated; HPLC anthocyanin quantification; purple endosperm phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsANS was transferred because endogenous OsANS was not expressed in wild-type or PER endosperm. 2. Its transgenic expression completed the late anthocyanidin-forming step of the pathway. 3. This enabled accumulation of C3G and P3G in engineered rice endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Important complementation gene for anthocyanin biofortification in cereal endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of stacked transgene set associated with ~25% reduced grain weight in PER lines; gene-specific effect not isolated
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at 7 and 14 DAP; endosperm-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data used for endogenous pathway context; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly states OsANS was not activated by the regulatory genes in both ZH11 and PER-Z#3, justifying need for SsANS.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice grains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) under endosperm-specific promoter
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter (distinct ESP used in stacked construct; exact promoter for this gene not specified)
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endogenous anthocyanin biosynthesis genes in rice endosperm; OsWD40; pathway genes including OsCHS, OsCHI, OsF3'H and additional upstream phenylalanine, decorating, and transporter genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or enhances expression of 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes together with ZmPl and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and associated decoration/transport in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via rebuilt MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct/indirect unresolved; acts as MBW complex regulator to activate pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Transgenic endosperm-specific expression in engineered construct
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for anthocyanin accumulation and purple endosperm formation; promotes high anthocyanin content and antioxidant activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator; required component of engineered MBW complex for anthocyanin production in rice endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression in rice; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; phenotype-genotype association in lines lacking ZmLc that produced white grains; HPLC anthocyanin profiling; TEAC antioxidant assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmLc was one of two maize regulatory genes introduced to activate anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. In PER lines, transgene expression coincided with activation/upregulation of 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes. 3. Lines lacking only ZmLc produced white grains, confirming ZmLc is required for engineered anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for biofortification of rice endosperm with anthocyanins and for engineering complex flavonoid traits in cereals
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Transgenic PER lines showed about 25% reduced grain weight relative to ZH11; gene-specific tradeoff not separated
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at 7 and 14 DAP under transgenic endosperm-specific expression
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analyzed for endogenous rice anthocyanin-pathway expression; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene source species differs from study species. ZmLc complements defective/silent endogenous regulatory context in rice endosperm and works with endogenous OsWD40; OsC1 was not activated.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm of developing and mature rice grains
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB-type transcription factor; MBW complex component; homolog of ZmC1 with stronger activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: High transgene expression in developing endosperm (7 and 14 days after pollination) under endosperm-specific promoter
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: Endosperm-specific promoter (distinct ESP used in stacked construct; exact promoter for this gene not specified)
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Endogenous anthocyanin biosynthesis genes in rice endosperm; OsWD40; pathway genes including OsCHS, OsCHI, OsF3'H and additional upstream phenylalanine, decorating, and transporter genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or enhances expression of 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes together with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and associated decoration/transport in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via rebuilt MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct/indirect unresolved; acts as MBW complex regulator to activate pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Transgenic endosperm-specific expression in engineered construct
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and purple endosperm formation as part of engineered regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key activator in engineered MBW complex enabling anthocyanin pathway activation in rice endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression in rice; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; HPLC anthocyanin profiling; TEAC antioxidant assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPl was selected as a strong MYB regulator to activate anthocyanin biosynthesis in rice endosperm. 2. Its expression in PER lines coincided with activation/upregulation of 13 endogenous anthocyanin-related genes together with ZmLc. 3. ZmPl functioned with ZmLc and endogenous OsWD40 to rebuild an MBW complex that enabled purple endosperm formation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful for anthocyanin biofortification and synthetic reconstruction of flavonoid regulatory networks in cereal endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Transgenic PER lines showed about 25% reduced grain weight relative to ZH11; gene-specific tradeoff not separated
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm at 7 and 14 DAP under transgenic endosperm-specific expression
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Public transcriptome data analyzed for endogenous rice anthocyanin-pathway expression; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene source species differs from study species. Authors note ZmPl is a homolog of ZmC1 but has stronger activity. OsC1 was not activated in PER.

Title: Development of “Purple Endosperm Rice” by Engineering Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in the Endosperm with a High-Efficiency Transgene Stacking System
